semantic.role: File-based distributed mutex utility ensuring exclusive access to the ArchSpine runtime directory across concurrent processes.
responsibilities: Manage file-based lock files under the .spine directory to prevent concurrent access conflicts.
Acquire locks with retry logic (MAX_LOCK_RETRIES) and token generation using randomUUID.
Release locks and enforce cleanup on graceful shutdown, process exit, and uncaught exceptions via process event handlers.
Serialize and deserialize lock payloads (token + timestamp) for persistence and inspection.
Provide parse/serialize utility functions for external consumers to read or write lock files.
outOfScope: Deadlock detection beyond simple retry count.
Semantic interpretation of lock payload content beyond token and timestamp.
invariants: At most one process can hold the lock for a given .spine directory at any moment.
Lock files are always cleaned up when a process releases the lock or exits.
A lock token is a UUID unique per acquisition attempt.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a lightweight, file-based mutual exclusion primitive for the ArchSpine CLI and services that require exclusive access to shared state (e.g., manifest updates, runtime checkpoints).
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: No specific recent changes detected beyond general maintenance; the lock module remains stable as a core infrastructure utility.

publicSurface.description: Class that acquires and releases file-based locks on the .spine directory. Used by any ArchSpine component that needs exclusive access to the runtime environment.
Parses a JSON string into a RuntimeLockPayload object. Enables external code to inspect the content of an existing lock file.
Serializes a RuntimeLockPayload object into a JSON string. Used to write lock files with the correct payload structure.
Interface defining the shape of a lock payload (token and timestamp). Implemented by lock files and used by consumers creating or validating locks.
